label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to work through this problem step by step. So there's a fruit stand that sells five fruits: apples, oranges, watermelons, pears, and mangoes. We need to figure out their order of prices based on the given statements, and then determine which of the options is correct regarding the second-most expensive fruit. First, let me list out the given information: 1. Apples are more expensive than oranges. (A > O) 2. Oranges are more expensive than mangoes. (O > M) 3. Apples are the third-most expensive. So in the order from most to least expensive, apples are in position 3. 4. Pears are more expensive than watermelons. (P > W) Our goal is to determine the correct order
